note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves the use of transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) to investigate the effects of different electrode sizes and stimulation conditions on motor evoked potentials (MEPs). The goal is to determine how the size of the electrodes influences the focal effect of tDCS.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A person is seated in a chair while electrodes are attached to their scalp using a helmet.",
    "Electrodes are placed at specific locations on the scalp as indicated by a diagram showing the DLPFC position.",
    "The experimenter adjusts settings on a stimulator machine, including intensity and duration.",
    "The subject is then stimulated with either active or sham tDCS."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Electrodes",
    "Stimulator machine",
    "Helmet for electrode attachment",
    "Subject (person)"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Stimulator machine with digital display and control knobs",
    "Diagram showing electrode placement",
    "Helmet with pre-attached electrodes"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Bar graph comparing MEP size in % after stimulation for different electrode sizes and stimulation conditions."
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Transition from attaching electrodes to adjusting stimulator settings",
    "Transition from active stimulation to sham stimulation"
  ],
  "outcomes_or_indicators": [
    "Bar graph showing MEP size differences between active and sham stimulation",
    "Graph indicating the relationship between electrode size and focal effect of tDCS"
  ],
  "anything_unusual_or_notable": [
    "Use of a helmet with pre-attached electrodes",
    "Detailed diagrams showing electrode placement and stimulation parameters"
  ]
}
